statistical_sentences: All models have N = 256 cortical neurons and M = 32 thalamic neurons.To begin, we took an approach known as meta-learning or “learning to learn” (Wang, 2021; Hospedales et al., 2022).
All models have N = 256 cortical units.
Error bars denote ±1 SEM. (Motor control: n=3 sessions, Working memory: n=5).

Error bars in D and E denote standard errors in mean (Motor control: n=101, Working memory: n=72).We found that behavior was well explained by cortical activity in both tasks (R2 — Motor control: 0.93, Working memory: 0.97).

We found that we could capture a substantial fraction of thalamic variance in both tasks, although the fraction was higher in the working memory task (Motor control: 0.46 ± 0.01, Working memory: 0.70 ± 0.01; Figure S5D).

The fraction of explainable variance in thalamus captured by the top 5 cortical PCs was significantly greater during the working memory task than motor control (Motor control: 0.29 ± 0.02, Working memory: 0.63 ± 0.04).Further analysis suggested that corticothalamic interactions identified above are not solely due to common input to cortex and thalamus.
All models have N = 256 cortical neurons and M = 32 thalamic neurons.
Error bars in D and E denote standard errors in mean (Motor control: n=101, Working memory: n=72).
